## Riot Relay 1.4.3

This release adds a fully custom-branded installer as an alternative to the plain NSIS wizard.

### Custom installer
- A new `Riot-Relay-Installer-1.4.3-x64.exe` is now published alongside the existing NSIS and MSI installers. It's a small, separate app that shows a bespoke welcome → license → install location → progress → finish wizard instead of the default NSIS UI.
- It installs per-user (no admin rights, no elevation prompt), creates the same Desktop/Start Menu shortcuts, and registers properly in Windows' "Apps & Features" so it can be uninstalled the normal way — or by running it again with a matching custom uninstall wizard.
- This does not change how updates work: Riot Relay still checks for and applies updates the same way as before, silently in the background. The custom installer only changes what the first-time install/uninstall experience looks like.
- The existing `Riot-Relay-Setup-*.exe` (NSIS) and `Riot-Relay-*.msi` downloads are unchanged and still published for anyone who prefers them.

### Validation
- All 78 automated tests pass.
- The installer's actual install/uninstall logic (file extraction, shortcut creation, Windows registry "Apps & Features" entry, and cleanup) was verified against the real Windows registry and filesystem APIs in an isolated test run, separate from the main test suite.
- JavaScript syntax checks and diagnostics pass on all changed and new files.
- Both installer builds (main app + custom installer) were built and launched successfully.

### Downloads
- **Installer (custom):** `Riot-Relay-Installer-1.4.3-x64.exe` — the new bespoke wizard.
- **EXE / NSIS:** `Riot-Relay-Setup-1.4.3-x64.exe` — the previous default installer, still available.
- **MSI:** intended for managed or manual deployment.
- `SHA256SUMS.txt` contains SHA-256 hashes for published assets.

Riot Relay is unofficial community software and is not endorsed by Riot Games.
